summary:
Resorption primarily facilitates arch space formation; however, multiple processes including soft-tissue changes contribute collectively during second and third molar eruptions. To directly answer your query based strictly upon these core elements without extraneous detail, focus would be best placed solely on 'bone resorption' being crucial here among all mentioned options provided initially. Agent2 concludes that although various biological processes work together, bone resorption stands out as critical for creating sufficient room within the oral cavity specifically when discussing secondary or tertiary molar emergence due largely but not exclusively because of how jaws grow vertically over time too impacting overall alignment dynamics post-resorptive events have occurred first prompting further development stages thereafter sequentially leading up until final occlusal relationships stabilize naturally after years typically pass under normal circumstances barring any anomalies present requiring intervention otherwise known clinically speaking across disciplines today regarding orthodontics/periodontics etcetera...
